title: Call of Cthulhu (7th Edition)
resolution_core: d100 roll-under skill with Hard (half) and Extreme (fifth) thresholds (BRP)

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.roll_under_perc: Every skill and characteristic is a percentile rating; investigators roll d100 and succeed by rolling at or under it, the classic Basic Roleplaying engine Chaosium built the game on.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.degrees_of_success: 7e formalizes three success levels printed on the sheet: Regular (under skill), Hard (under half), and Extreme (under one-fifth), with Keepers setting which level a task demands.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.advantage_disadvant: Instead of percentage modifiers, circumstances grant bonus or penalty dice: roll one or two extra tens dice and keep the best (bonus) or worst (penalty) tens result.
vector_explanations.luck.push.escalating_risk: A failed skill roll may be pushed - retried once with in-fiction justification - but a failed pushed roll hands the Keeper license to inflict a dire consequence, and insane investigators suffer even worse outcomes; combat and Sanity rolls can never be pushed.
vector_explanations.resolution.luck.luck_stat: Luck is rolled at creation (3d6 x 5) as its own percentile score; the Keeper calls for Luck rolls when pure chance, not skill, decides whether the cultists left the door unlocked.
vector_explanations.resolution.luck.luck_burn: The core spending-Luck rule lets players deplete their Luck score point-for-point to convert failed skill rolls into successes, trading long-term fortune for immediate survival.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.opposed_rolls: Contests such as Psychology versus a liar's Fast Talk have both sides roll and compare achieved success levels (Regular/Hard/Extreme), with the higher level winning and ties broken by skill rating.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.sanity_meter: Every investigator has a 0-99 Sanity score starting at POW; encounters with the Mythos drain it via rolled losses, and it is the game's true hit-point track.
vector_explanations.character.progression.sanity_loss: Long-term play is charted in decline: losses are written in the iconic notation (0/1d4, 1/1d6, 1d10/1d100), meaning the loss on a successful versus failed SAN roll, and most losses never come back.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.cthulhu_mythos_exposure: Points gained in the Cthulhu Mythos skill permanently lower maximum Sanity to 99 minus Cthulhu Mythos, so learning the truth of the universe directly caps mental health.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.temporary_insanity: Losing 5 or more Sanity from a single roll forces an INT roll; success means the investigator fully comprehends what they saw and suffers a bout of madness lasting minutes or hours.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.indefinite_insanity: Losing one-fifth of current Sanity within a single day inflicts indefinite insanity, an underlying disorder that persists across scenarios until treated in downtime.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.sanity_zero_removal: An investigator whose Sanity reaches 0 is permanently, incurably insane and passes out of the player's hands, one of the game's explicit end states alongside death.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.delusions_unreliable_narra: While insane, the Keeper may feed the player false sensory information; the investigator can attempt a reality check roll to pierce the delusion, and failure deepens the false narrative.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.mythos_tome_study: Reading forbidden tomes is a priced transaction with initial and full-study phases, each costing rolled Sanity while granting Cthulhu Mythos percentiles and possible spells.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.madness_tables: Bouts of madness are rolled on dedicated 1d10 tables - Real Time (amnesia, violence, hysteria) or Summary for episodes that occur between scenes - rather than chosen by the Keeper.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.phobias_manias: The rulebook carries long alphabetical lists of phobias and manias; bouts of madness and insane fumbles can saddle an investigator with a specific one that thereafter imposes penalty dice when triggered.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.disorders_treatment: Indefinite insanity is treated over downtime through psychoanalysis (its own skill, which can claw back Sanity points) or months of institutional or private care, at cost in time and money.
vector_explanations.character.skills.percentile_improvement_: Skills used successfully during a scenario are checked; in the improvement phase the player rolls over the current rating on d100 to add 1d10 points, so ability grows from practice.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Characteristics are rolled - 3d6 x 5 for STR, CON, DEX, APP, and POW, and 2d6+6 x 5 for SIZ, INT, and EDU - producing percentile-scale stats with built-in unevenness.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.derived_attributes: Hit points are (CON+SIZ)/10, magic points POW/5, starting Sanity equals POW, and STR+SIZ set Build and Damage Bonus, so the sheet's secondary numbers all flow from characteristics by formula.
vector_explanations.character.progression.professions: Occupations (Antiquarian, Journalist, Private Investigator) define which eight skills receive occupational points from formulas like EDU x 2 + STR x 2 and set the allowable Credit Rating range, tying competence to trade.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.grappling: Fighting maneuvers cover disarms, pins, trips, and seizing items as opposed Fighting rolls, with each point of Build difference between combatants adding a penalty die and a gap over two making the maneuver impossible.
vector_explanations.combat.melee.percentage_opposed: Melee is opposed percentile rolling: the defender declares dodge or fight back, both sides roll, and success levels are compared - fighting back can wound the attacker while dodging cannot.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Damage depletes a hit-point pool of (CON+SIZ)/10; at 0 hit points the investigator is dying and must receive First Aid, then survive CON rolls until stabilized.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.massive_damage: A single hit equal to or exceeding half maximum hit points is a Major Wound: the investigator falls prone, must pass a CON roll to stay conscious, and cannot heal it by natural first aid alone.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Firearms list a base range; shots out to double base range are Hard and out to quadruple are Extreme, so distance steps the required success level rather than applying flat modifiers.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Combat order runs from highest to lowest DEX, with readied firearms acting ahead of their DEX rank, and no initiative roll at all.
vector_explanations.stealth.pursuit.chase_rules: A dedicated chase chapter builds pursuit as a track of locations: participants establish speed with CON/MOV rolls, spend movement actions, and face rolled hazards and barriers until escape or capture.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.wealth_levels: Credit Rating is a skill that fixes the investigator's spending level, cash, and assets by era-specific table, so purchases compare cost against a rating instead of counting dollars.
vector_explanations.magic.risk.sanity_cost: Casting Mythos spells costs magic points and, almost always, Sanity - Summon Deity class effects can demand 1d10 SAN or more - pricing sorcery in the caster's mind.

title: Delta Green
resolution_core: d100 roll-under skill or characteristic x 5; matched digits crit

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.roll_under_perc: Agents test d100 against skill ratings or characteristic x 5 targets; rolling at or under succeeds, keeping the whole game on one percentile engine descended from Call of Cthulhu.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_success: Rolls with matching digits are special: doubles at or under the target (or an 01) are critical successes that double damage or effect, while matching failures (or 100) are fumbles that make everything worse.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.opposed_rolls: Contests like a struggle over a gun or Persuade against an interrogator's suspicion have both sides roll under their ratings, with the higher successful roll winning the exchange.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.sanity_meter: Sanity starts at POW x 5 and is drained by violence, helplessness, and the unnatural in rolled increments; it is the resource the whole campaign is designed to grind down.
vector_explanations.character.progression.sanity_loss: Losses use the 0/1d4 and 1/1d10 notation (loss on a passed versus failed SAN roll), and the long arc of an agent is measured in what those losses take from family, career, and self.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.breaking_point: Each agent has a Breaking Point equal to SAN minus POW; when Sanity falls to it, the agent snaps, acquires a new disorder, and resets the Breaking Point lower - a codified staircase of psychological collapse.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.sanity_bonds: When losing Sanity, an agent may project the trauma onto a Bond, reducing the personal SAN loss by 1d4 but stripping the same amount from the relationship - the game's signature trade of family for function.
vector_explanations.character.identity.bonds_relationships: Bonds - spouse, children, partners - are scored on the sheet at values derived from CHA; they fray as the agent projects trauma or neglects home, and a Bond at 0 is a relationship destroyed.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.hardened_desensitization: After enough temporary insanities from violence or helplessness (three incidents), an agent becomes adapted: immune to further SAN loss from that source, but visibly deadened and prone to losing Bonds faster.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.temporary_insanity: Losing 5 or more SAN at once triggers temporary insanity - flight, paralysis, submission, or frenzy chosen from codified reactions - during which the Handler may dictate the agent's actions.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.cthulhu_mythos_exposure: The Unnatural skill represents forbidden understanding; every point of it lowers maximum Sanity (99 minus Unnatural), so studying the enemy permanently shrinks the mind's ceiling.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.disorders_treatment: Disorders gained at Breaking Points (depression, paranoia, addiction) have explicit descriptions and can be sent into remission through therapy sessions between operations, though the unnatural can reawaken them.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.mythos_tome_study: Studying unnatural texts costs Sanity and study time in exchange for Unnatural percentiles and rituals, making each tome a priced bargain the rules spell out per book.
vector_explanations.character.progression.skill_failure_tick: Skills that fail during an operation get a checkmark; after the operation each checked skill improves, so agents learn from the missions that went wrong rather than from triumphs.
vector_explanations.character.progression.professions: Professional backgrounds (Federal Agent, Physician, Special Operator) fix the agent's starting skill package and bonus-skill picks, grounding competence in a pre-Delta-Green career.
vector_explanations.downtime.structure.downtime_phases: Between operations each agent chooses one personal pursuit from a menu - fulfilling responsibilities to a Bond, establishing a new Bond, undergoing therapy, or training - making home life a formal phase of play.
vector_explanations.combat.firearms.lethality_rating: Heavy weapons carry a Lethality percentage; on a hit the attacker rolls it, and success kills the target outright while failure still converts the roll's digits into damage - the mechanic this vector is named for.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: The default build distributes 72 points across six characteristics (or takes a published array), each multiplied by 5 for its percentile test target, trading breadth against peaks.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.derived_attributes: Hit points are (STR+CON)/2, Willpower Points equal POW, Sanity is POW x 5, and the Breaking Point is SAN minus POW - the sheet's key thresholds all derive from characteristics.
vector_explanations.magic.risk.sanity_cost: Every genuine ritual erodes the operator: activations burn Willpower Points and cost Sanity, so hypergeometry is deliberately priced as self-destruction rather than a power fantasy.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.ritual_casting: There are no combat spells, only rituals learned from tomes; each demands time, components, WP expenditure, and sometimes ceremony with multiple participants, per the Handler's Guide ritual chapter.

title: Trail of Cthulhu
resolution_core: GUMSHOE: investigative clues are automatic; general tests roll 1d6 + pool spends vs Difficulty (usually 4)

vector_explanations.simulation.investigation.gumshoe_auto_cl: The founding GUMSHOE game: any investigator with the right investigative ability (Archaeology, Cop Talk) automatically finds every core clue in a scene, while 1-2 point pool spends buy extra revelations and advantages.
vector_explanations.simulation.investigation.mystery_structu: Scenarios are published as webs of scenes, each keyed with the core clue that leads onward plus optional spends, so the mystery is structurally guaranteed never to stall on a failed roll.
vector_explanations.character.progression.gumshoe_points: Characters are built from build points (the investigative budget scales with the number of players) and earn more between cases to deepen investigative and general ability pools.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.no_attributes: There are no attribute scores at all: an investigator is entirely defined by rated investigative and general abilities, GUMSHOE's signature ability-only design.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.static_dc: General tests roll a single d6 against a Difficulty that defaults to 4, with players choosing how many pool points to add before rolling - tension comes from budgeting, not from the die.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.sanity_stability: Mental state is split across two pools: Stability is short-term composure lost in frightening scenes and recoverable, while Sanity is the deep reservoir eroded by the Mythos itself and essentially gone for good - the design this vector describes.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.sanity_meter: Sanity is a small pool (capped by 10 minus Cthulhu Mythos) drained specifically by confronting Mythos truths; hitting 0 removes the investigator into madness, denial, or worse.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.cthulhu_mythos_exposure: Every rating point in the Cthulhu Mythos ability permanently lowers maximum Sanity, so the investigator best equipped to understand the horror is closest to being destroyed by it.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.fear_tests: Frightening events force Stability tests - 1d6 plus optional Stability spends against Difficulty 4 - with losses scaled by the severity of the shock, from corpses up to the gods themselves.
vector_explanations.character.identity.values_drives: Each investigator has a Drive (Curiosity, Duty, Revenge) that compels them toward the mystery; resisting your Drive when it applies costs Stability, mechanically pushing characters into the dark house.
vector_explanations.character.identity.beliefs_instincts: Pillars of Sanity are written convictions - faith, rationalism, love of family - that anchor the mind; when the Mythos shatters a Pillar, the investigator suffers deep Sanity loss the rules direct at exactly those beliefs.
vector_explanations.social.relationships.bond_mechanics: Sources of Stability are named individuals (up to three); spending time with them between adventures restores Stability, and their corruption or death is a codified route to psychological ruin.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.mythos_tome_study: Poring over Mythos tomes grants Cthulhu Mythos rating and potential spells while costing Stability and threatening Sanity, with the trade priced per text.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.disorders_treatment: Falling to the bottom of the Stability track inflicts mental illness chosen from period-appropriate disorders, treatable only through long downtime care such as sanitarium stays.
vector_explanations.character.progression.professions: Occupations (Alienist, Antiquarian, Private Investigator) discount occupational abilities and define Credit Rating ranges, rooting each investigator in a 1930s trade.
vector_explanations.narrative.tone.tone_dials: The book explicitly runs in two switchable modes, Purist and Pulp, which change Sanity harshness, death rules, and even which abilities exist - a tone dial baked into the rules text.
vector_explanations.magic.risk.sanity_cost: Casting Mythos rituals is priced in the caster's own mind and body, draining Stability and often Sanity or Health, so magic is a losing bargain by design.
vector_explanations.combat.melee.scuffling: Close combat uses the Scuffling general ability: a limited pool spent to boost simple d6 attack rolls, modeling how quickly bookish investigators run out of fight.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Health is a pool; at 0 or below the investigator is hurt and takes penalties, at -6 seriously wounded and making consciousness rolls, and at -12 dead - a graded attrition ladder.
vector_explanations.equipment.economy_of_gear.quick_equipmen: The Preparedness general ability lets a player test to have retroactively packed exactly the right equipment, replacing shopping lists with a dice-backed flashback.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.group_checks: Piggybacking rules let one skilled character make a group test (like sneaking past cultists) while every other participant pays a point into the effort or risks spoiling it.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.wealth_levels: Credit Rating is an ability rating standing in for wealth and class; purchases and lifestyle are adjudicated against it rather than tracked in dollars.

title: Vaesen: Nordic Horror Roleplaying
resolution_core: d6 dice pool (attribute + skill), 6s are successes; push once by taking a Condition (Year Zero Engine)

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.dice_pool_succe: Tests roll a pool of d6s equal to attribute plus skill; every 6 is a success, and most tasks need only one, in Free League's Year Zero Engine idiom.
vector_explanations.luck.mitigation.dice_rerolls: You may push a failed roll once, rerolling every die that is not a 6 - but the price is taking a physical or mental Condition, so desperation is written straight onto the character sheet.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.condition_penalties: Conditions come in two ladders - physical (Exhausted, Battered, Wounded) and mental (Angry, Frightened, Hopeless) - each imposing -1 to relevant rolls; checking all three of a type leaves you Broken.
vector_explanations.combat.damage.critical_injury: Being Broken forces a roll on the physical or mental critical injury tables, whose entries range from lingering wounds to permanent traumas like nightmares or a fear of the dark.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Characters are built from archetypes - Academic, Doctor, Hunter, Occultist, Priest, Servant, Vagabond, Writer - which package a main skill, starting Talent, Resources range, and equipment.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: Players distribute a budget of points across the four attributes (Physique, Precision, Logic, Empathy) within archetype limits, setting the base size of every dice pool.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_ranks: Twelve skills are bought in ranks from 0 to 5 and added to attributes when building pools, with the archetype's main skill allowed to start higher than the rest.
vector_explanations.downtime.projects.base_improvements: Between mysteries the Society spends earned development points upgrading Castle Gyllencreutz - unlocking Facilities, Contacts, and Personnel that grant concrete bonuses on future mysteries - the headquarters subsystem the game is known for.
vector_explanations.downtime.structure.downtime_phases: Play formally alternates between mysteries and a structured interlude at headquarters where characters heal Conditions, develop the castle, and advance, before the next invitation arrives.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.story_xp_triggers: Experience is awarded by answering end-of-session questions - did you participate, did you learn something about the vaesen, did you risk something for another - tying advancement to fiction rather than kills.
vector_explanations.simulation.investigation.mystery_structu: Published mysteries follow a fixed anatomy - prelude, arrival, locations seeded with clues, and confrontation - so investigation always has multiple threads leading toward the vaesen's secret.
vector_explanations.politics.factions.front_countdown: Every mystery includes a Countdown: a numbered sequence of escalating catastrophes the vaesen causes if the player characters dally, firing step by step until they intervene.
vector_explanations.simulation.investigation.clues: Mystery locations are keyed with discoverable clues about the creature's nature, victims, and weakness; gathering them is what converts a haunting from unwinnable to solvable.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.ritual_casting: Vaesen cannot simply be shot: each creature's entry names the specific ritual or condition that can bind, banish, or kill it, and enacting that ritual is the climax the investigation builds toward.
vector_explanations.turns.order.card_determined_initiative: Combat order comes from drawing numbered initiative cards (1-10) at the start of the fight, with some Talents letting quick characters swap for better draws.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: XP earned from session questions is spent directly to raise skills or buy new Talents at listed costs, a slow point-buy advancement between mysteries.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Talents are discrete named perks - archetype-specific tricks and general abilities - that grant bonus dice or new options in defined situations, purchased as characters advance.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.wealth_levels: Each character has a Resources rating set by archetype; buying gear compares an item's listed cost against Resources instead of counting coins, with upkeep tied to the rating.
vector_explanations.character.identity.flaws_drawbacks: Every character carries a Dark Secret chosen at creation - a haunting guilt or hidden shame the GM is instructed to drag into mysteries as pressure and complication.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.relationshi: Creation ends by defining written relationships to every other player character plus a Motivation, wiring the Society together with tensions before the first mystery begins.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.help_dice: Characters who can plausibly assist add bonus dice to the acting character's pool before the roll, letting the Society pool its strength against the supernatural.

title: Ten Candles
resolution_core: Communal d6 pool equal to lit candles; any 6 succeeds; a failed roll darkens a candle and ends the scene

vector_explanations.meta.structure_tools.candle_timekeeping: Ten real tea-light candles structure the whole game: scenes end when a candle is darkened, the players' dice pool always equals the number of still-burning candles, and the story is over when the last flame dies.
vector_explanations.meta.real_time.real_time_timers: The candles burn in actual time and can gutter out on their own, ending scenes regardless of what the characters were doing - the session literally races the wax.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.dread_escalation: Dread is mechanized as loss: each darkened candle shrinks the communal pool and grows the GM's, so the odds visibly worsen scene by scene toward inevitable collapse.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.environmental_dread: The setting's premise is codified - ten days of worldwide darkness and Them who hunt within it - and the rules keep light itself the only safety, played out in a room lit by nothing but the candles.
vector_explanations.mental.composure.hope_dice: Living out your Moment ('I will find hope in the eyes of a stranger') earns the Hope Die, a persistent extra die that stays with the character and resists the pool's decay - the game's only upward mechanic.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.dice_pool_succe: Conflicts roll the communal pool of d6s; any 6 succeeds. Dice that show 1s are set aside for the rest of the scene, so even successful scenes grind the pool down.
vector_explanations.luck.mitigation.dice_rerolls: Burning a Virtue or Vice card narrates that trait into the scene and rerolls the 1s just lost; once every card in the stack is burned, only the Brink remains between the character and the dark.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.relationshi: Trait cards are written for neighbors around the table - and your Brink is authored by the player beside you as something they have seen you do - entangling every character in others' eyes before play begins.
vector_explanations.character.identity.values_drives: Each player writes a Moment, a concrete statement of where their character will find hope; pursuing and succeeding at it is the defined route to the Hope Die.
vector_explanations.character.identity.flaws_drawbacks: The Brink is a dark truth at the bottom of your card stack ('I've seen you kill'); embracing it lets you reroll your entire pool, but failure burns the card and the self it described.
vector_explanations.narrative.worldbuilding.setting_truths_s: At the top of each scene the table performs the Establishing Truths ritual - declaring one binding truth per darkened candle, closing with 'and we are alive' - collectively authoring the world's decay.
vector_explanations.narrative.structure.endgame_triggers: When only one candle remains the game enters its defined final phase: rolls that fail now kill characters one by one until the last flame gutters and everyone still alive dies in the dark.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: Character death is a certainty stated on page one; the rules govern its timing - deaths are narrated when final-scene rolls fail - making the game about how, not whether, these people die.
vector_explanations.narrative.tone.horror_atmosphere_rules: The game mandates its own ritual staging: lights off, ten candles lit one by one during character creation, truths spoken aloud - procedures that manufacture dread at the table beyond anything on a sheet.
vector_explanations.narrative.structure.epilogues: Before play, everyone records a short goodbye message from their character; after the last candle dies, the recordings are played back into the darkened room as the game's built-in epilogue.
vector_explanations.narrative.authority.shared_narration: Narrative control is deliberately distributed: players take turns establishing truths, narrate their own successes, and cede narration to the GM when rolls fail and the dark answers.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.no_attributes: Characters have no statistics whatsoever - they are a name and a stack of index cards (Virtue, Vice, Moment, Brink) that are burned away over the course of the night.

title: Dread
resolution_core: Pull blocks from a Jenga tower to succeed; refusing fails safely, toppling the tower removes the character

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.jenga_tower: The game's entire resolution engine is a Jenga tower: any risky action requires pulling a block and placing it on top; a completed pull succeeds, a refused pull fails safely, and a toppled tower removes the character from the story.
vector_explanations.meta.structure_tools.dread_tower_pulls: Pulls accumulate: every extracted block leaves the tower objectively less stable for everyone who follows, making shared instability the game's central prop and pacing device.
vector_explanations.combat.melee.jenga_tower_pull: Violence has no separate subsystem - fighting the horror means pulls, and desperate physical struggles typically demand several in succession, so combat is exactly as terrifying as the tower is tall.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.dread_escalation: Dread mounts physically rather than numerically: as the tower leans, hands genuinely shake, and the rules lean on that somatic tension as the game's horror engine.
vector_explanations.luck.push.escalating_risk: Each successive pull carries a higher chance of collapse than the last because safe blocks deplete - the purest possible implementation of escalating push-your-luck.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.questionnai: Characters are created entirely by answering a host-written questionnaire (around a dozen questions per character); the answers establish skills, history, and fears without a single number.
vector_explanations.narrative.prompts.leading_questions: The questionnaires are deliberately leading - 'Why do you still blame yourself for your brother's death?' - embedding presuppositions the player must accept and elaborate, and the host may pose new binding questions during play.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.no_attributes: There are no stats, skills ratings, or dice anywhere in the system; what a character can plausibly do flows solely from their questionnaire answers and the fiction.
vector_explanations.character.progression.none: Dread has no advancement mechanics of any kind - capabilities are fixed by the questionnaire for the whole story, in keeping with its one-shot horror-movie structure.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: Knocking the tower over is the codified death rule: the character is removed from the story - killed, lost, or worse - and the tower is rebuilt for the survivors.
vector_explanations.cooperation.assistance.rescue_mechanics: A player may deliberately topple the tower as a sacrifice: the character is removed but the attempted action succeeds, letting one doomed soul buy the others' survival - an explicit rule, not table fiat.
vector_explanations.narrative.tone.horror_atmosphere_rules: The rulebook devotes chapters to hosting horror - pacing scenes around the tower's state, exploiting the questionnaire's confessions - and the looming tower itself functions as mandated table atmosphere.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.variable_tn: Difficulty is dialed by the number of pulls demanded: trivial feats need none, hard ones a single pull, and desperate or extended feats two or three consecutive pulls, raising the bar directly.
vector_explanations.meta.structure_tools.tension_tracker: The tower is a shared, visible tension meter that changes the game as it climbs toward collapse - every consequence gets harsher because the same wobbling structure adjudicates it.
vector_explanations.campaign.continuity.replacement_characte: The book covers folding in new and replacement characters after a removal - abbreviated questionnaires bring latecomers in mid-story so the horror never has to pause for bookkeeping.
vector_explanations.meta.player_facing.player_facing_rolls: All risk sits with the players: NPCs and the menace never pull; threats are expressed entirely as demands for player pulls, keeping every moment of jeopardy in player hands.
vector_explanations.meta.player_facing.gm_never_rolls: The host uses no randomizer at all - their job is framing scenes, asking questions, and deciding when a pull is required, adjudicating purely through fiction and the tower.
vector_explanations.character.identity.flaws_drawbacks: Questionnaires deliberately install weaknesses, secrets, and guilt ('What do you carry that you could never explain?') that the host is instructed to weaponize as the horror closes in.

title: Mothership
resolution_core: d100 roll-under stat or Save; doubles are criticals; failure feeds Stress and Panic

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.roll_under_perc: Everything tests d100 against Stats (Strength, Speed, Intellect, Combat) or Saves, succeeding at or under the target; skills add flat bonuses to the target number.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.advantage_disadvant: Favorable or dire circumstances impose Advantage or Disadvantage, rolling the percentile dice twice and keeping the better or worse result instead of stacking modifiers.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_failure: Doubles are criticals: matched digits under the target are critical successes, while matched digits over it are critical failures that immediately demand a Panic Check - dice math wired directly into the horror loop.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.stress_track: Every character accumulates Stress from failures, darkness, and gore onto a numeric track with a resting floor it never fully drops below; Stress is the fuel every Panic Check burns.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.panic_rolls: At crisis moments characters make a Panic Check against accumulated Stress; failing consults the escalating Panic Effect Table, whose results run from trembling and paralysis up to heart attack at the top of the table.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.stress_from_failure: Failed Saves and checks automatically add Stress, so incompetence under pressure compounds - the game's core death spiral of the mind.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.panic_contagion: Panic spreads by rule: a Marine's panic forces nearby crew to make Fear Saves of their own, letting one scream cascade down the whole corridor.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.sanctuary_recovery: Stress is only meaningfully shed in safety - shore leave and downtime in port between jobs - and botched relief can leave lasting marks, making sanctuary a scarce resource on a derelict.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.trauma_conditions: The worst panic results inscribe lasting psychological Conditions on the sheet - death wish, paranoia, catatonia - scars that persist until treated rather than fading with the scene.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Four classes - Marine, Android, Scientist, Teamster - set stat and Save adjustments plus a unique trauma response, so who you were hired as determines how you break.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Stats are rolled (2d10+25 in the original printing), embracing uneven crews of spacers rather than balanced adventurers.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Sanity, Fear, and Body Saves are separate rolled-under defenses (modified by class) that stand between the crew and the void; failing a Save is the main pipeline into Stress and Panic.
vector_explanations.character.skills.specialty_trees: Skills form prerequisite trees rated Trained, Expert, and Master with rising bonuses - you cannot take Surgery without the medical training beneath it - so expertise is a climbable lattice.
vector_explanations.combat.damage.injury_tables: Serious harm rolls on Wound tables split by damage type - blunt force, bleeding, gunshot, fire and explosives - layering concrete maimings on top of numeric damage.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Characters track a Health pool that damage erodes; emptying it inflicts Wounds and drops crew members toward death with very little of the safety margin adventure games assume.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.trinket_tables: Character creation famously ends with d100 rolls on the Trinket and Patch tables - a cigarette burned down to the filter, a 'MAMA TRIED' patch - pure evocative texture the rules encourage exploiting.
vector_explanations.equipment.economy_of_gear.starting_packa: Class loadout tables let players roll a complete starting kit - a Marine might draw a flak vest and SMG while a Teamster gets a rig and crowbar - skipping itemized shopping entirely.

title: Alien: The Roleplaying Game
resolution_core: d6 pool (attribute + skill + gear) plus Stress Dice; 6s succeed, 1s on Stress Dice trigger Panic (Year Zero Engine)

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.dice_pool_succe: Rolls build a pool of Base Dice from attribute plus skill plus gear; every 6 is a success, with extra sixes buying stunts - Free League's Year Zero Engine tuned for survival horror.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.stress_track: Each character carries a Stress Level from 0 to 10 that climbs from danger, pushing rolls, and firing on full auto, and everything about survival hinges on managing it.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.stress_dice_bonus: Stress is double-edged fuel: your current Stress Level adds that many yellow Stress Dice to every roll - more chances at sixes - but any 1 on a Stress Die (the facehugger symbol) forces an immediate Panic Roll.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.panic_rolls: Panic resolves as d6 plus Stress Level against the Panic Table: 7+ results escalate from Tremble and Drop Item through Scream and Flee up to Berserk and Catatonic, taking control away from the player.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.panic_contagion: Several panic results are explicitly infectious - a Scream or Flee raises the Stress of every friendly character who witnesses it, letting terror chain-react through the crew.
vector_explanations.luck.mitigation.dice_rerolls: You may push a roll once, rerolling non-sixes - but pushing always adds +1 Stress first, so second chances are bought with the very resource that triggers panic.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: Four attributes (Strength, Agility, Wits, Empathy) are bought from a point budget with career-keyed maximums, then paired with twelve skills bought the same way.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Careers - Colonial Marine, Company Agent, Pilot, Roughneck, Medic, Scientist, Kid, Officer - package key attributes, career skills, and starting talents into pickable archetypes.
vector_explanations.character.identity.values_drives: In Cinematic play each character holds a secret Personal Agenda card for the current act; pursuing it earns Story Points and regularly aims the crew at each other's throats.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.plot_points: Story Points, earned mainly by following Personal Agendas, are cashed in for an automatic success when the story absolutely demands survival or sabotage succeed.
vector_explanations.character.identity.bonds_relationships: Every character sheet names a Buddy and a Rival among the crew, wiring in loyalty and friction that the agenda system then pries at.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Talents are discrete named tricks (career-gated at creation, more bought later) granting rerolls, bonuses, or special moves in defined situations.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.story_xp_triggers: Campaign advancement asks end-of-session questions - did you risk something for your Buddy, did you advance your agenda - and pays XP for fiction hit, not aliens killed.
vector_explanations.combat.damage.critical_injury: Hitting zero Health makes you Broken and forces a d66 roll on the critical injury table, from winded to severed limbs to fatal wounds with countdown timers for treatment.
vector_explanations.turns.order.card_determined_initiative: Combat deals numbered initiative cards (1 to 10) to all combatants each fight; some talents and gear let you draw extra cards or swap, but the deck sets the order.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Distance uses Year Zero range bands - Engaged, Short, Medium, Long, Extreme - each stepping weapon usability and modifiers rather than counting meters.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.supply: Consumables (air, food, water, power) carry Supply ratings depleted by Supply Rolls: roll Stress Dice equal to the rating and each 1 knocks it down - so life support runs out exactly when tension peaks.
vector_explanations.stealth.detection.sensors: The motion tracker has dedicated device rules - detecting movement at range even through bulkheads on a successful Comtech roll, but blind to anything holding still - the franchise's dread machine mechanized.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.crew_positions: Spacecraft rules assign crew positions such as pilot, gunner, and engineer, each with its own actions during ship operations and combat encounters.
vector_explanations.narrative.structure.act_breaks: Cinematic scenarios are built in three acts; at each act break players receive new Personal Agenda cards and the scenario escalates, a codified structural seam in every published module.

title: KULT: Divinity Lost
resolution_core: 2d10 + attribute: 15+ full success, 10-14 success with complication, 9- the GM makes a Move (PbtA-derived)

vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.partial_success: Every move rolls 2d10 plus an attribute: 15+ is a clean success, 10-14 succeeds with a complication chosen from the move's list, and 9 or less invites disaster - the PbtA tier structure ported to 2d10.
vector_explanations.meta.player_facing.gm_never_rolls: Only players roll dice; the GM responds to results with narrative Moves, keeping every randomizer in player hands while the Illusion closes in.
vector_explanations.narrative.authority.fail_forward: A miss never means nothing happens - it licenses the GM to make a Move, so failed rolls always push the character deeper into the Truth behind the world.
vector_explanations.narrative.authority.gm_agenda_principles: The GM chapter binds narration to written agendas and principles in the Apocalypse World tradition, tuned here to personal horror: make the world feel authentic, then tear its mask away.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.playbook_ba: Characters are built from Archetypes - The Avenger, The Detective, The Occultist among the Aware - each a distinct booklet of attributes, Advantages, Disadvantages, and Dark Secret options.
vector_explanations.character.identity.playbook_moves: Advantages and Disadvantages function as archetype-specific moves with their own triggers and roll results, so what a character is and what they can do are the same list.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.standard_array: A fixed ladder of modifiers (from +2 down to -2) is distributed across the ten active attributes, guaranteeing every Aware starts competent somewhere and cracked somewhere else.
vector_explanations.character.identity.flaws_drawbacks: Every character carries a Dark Secret - Guilty of Crime, Victim of Medical Experiments, Returned from the Other Side - which the GM is explicitly instructed to build scenes around.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.relationshi: Creation establishes written Relations to the other player characters before play, seeding the web of attachments the horror will pull on.
vector_explanations.social.relationships.bond_mechanics: Relations are rated, and the strongest serve as Anchors: confiding in them is a codified path to regaining Stability, while their loss strikes directly at the mind.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.stability_points: Stability is a ladder from Composed down through increasingly frayed tiers to Broken, depleted by trauma and the Truth, with penalties mounting as it drops and recovery gated behind therapy and Anchors.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.madness_as_insight: The game's signature inversion: at low Stability the Illusion frays and characters See Through it more easily - losing your grip grants real mechanical perception of the world's hidden machinery.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.horror_checks: The Keep It Together move resolves confrontation with the terrifying: rolled with Willpower, its lower tiers impose reactions like freezing, fleeing, or lashing out and chip away Stability.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.nightmares_sleep_disru: Disadvantages like Nightmares are mechanized afflictions - rolled when you sleep, with failures bleeding dream horror into waking penalties - not just color.
vector_explanations.combat.melee.moves: Violence runs through moves such as Engage in Combat: 2d10 plus Violence, with full hits dealing harm cleanly and partial hits trading wounds or position rather than attack-versus-defense math.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.wound_levels: Damage marks a short ladder of Serious Wounds plus a single Critical Wound slot; wounds penalize rolls and a critical leaves the character dying without immediate aid.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.story_xp_triggers: Advancement flows from play milestones tied to your Archetype, Dark Secret, and Relations; accumulated XP buys picks from the archetype's advancement list, eventually opening awakened paths.
vector_explanations.meta.safety.content_calibration: The rulebook opens with explicit guidance on calibrating its transgressive content - discussing themes and boundaries with the group before and during play - as a formal part of running the game.

title: Vampire: The Masquerade 5th Edition
resolution_core: d10 pool (attribute + skill) vs Difficulty, 6+ = success; Hunger Dice replace normal dice and warp criticals and failures

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.dice_pool_succe: Tests roll d10 pools of attribute plus skill against a Difficulty in required successes, with 6+ counting as a success and pairs of 10s as criticals worth four - the Storyteller system's fifth-edition refit.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.dual_axis_outcomes: Hunger Dice substitute into every pool one-for-one with your Hunger: a 10 on a Hunger Die turns criticals Messy (the Beast overdoes it) and a 1 on a failed roll makes it a Bestial Failure - a second axis of outcome riding the same roll.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.addictions: Hunger 0-5 is dependency mechanized: it rises from Rouse Checks, can only be lowered by feeding on blood (and never below 1 except by draining a human), and at Hunger 5 the Beast sits at the table with you.
vector_explanations.mental.composure.emotional_states: Messy Criticals and Bestial Failures inflict Compulsions - Hunger, Dominance, Harm, Paranoia, or your clan's own - defined behavioral states with mechanical teeth until acted out.
vector_explanations.mental.composure.frenzy_loss_of_control: Provocation, hunger, or humiliation forces a Frenzy test (Willpower plus a third of Humanity); failure hands the character to the Beast for the scene, though you may deliberately Ride the Wave.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.fear_tests: Fire and sunlight trigger Terror Frenzy: a dedicated fear-frenzy test that, on failure, forces the vampire to flee mindlessly from the flame - Rötschreck codified.
vector_explanations.character.identity.splat_membership: Clan is the core template choice - Brujah, Gangrel, Malkavian, Nosferatu, Toreador, Tremere, Ventrue, thin-blood and more - fixing Disciplines, a clan Bane, and a clan Compulsion.
vector_explanations.character.identity.values_drives: Each character records Convictions, stated moral principles that can mitigate Stains when violence serves them, making personal ethics a live mechanical object.
vector_explanations.character.identity.bonds_relationships: Touchstones are living mortals tied to each Conviction who anchor the vampire's Humanity; endangering or losing one strikes directly at the Humanity system.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.corruption_track: Humanity 0-10 is the corruption track: violations of Chronicle Tenets mark Stains, an end-of-session Remorse roll decides whether Humanity drops, and low Humanity degrades what the character can even attempt.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.willpower_spend: Spending a point of Willpower lets you reroll up to three regular (never Hunger) dice, and Willpower doubles as the track social and mental damage depletes.
vector_explanations.mental.composure.willpower_recovery: Willpower returns through rest and through play - acting on Convictions and engaging Touchstones - so self-expression literally restores the capacity to resist.
vector_explanations.character.identity.backgrounds: Advantages such as Allies, Contacts, Resources, Haven, and Herd are dot-rated Backgrounds encoding the character's mortal-world reach, purchased at creation and pried at by the Storyteller.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Advancement spends XP on dots at listed multiplier costs, while Blood Potency climbs with age and generation to reshape what the Blood can do.
vector_explanations.magic.acquisition.bloodline_innate: Disciplines are inborn gifts of the Blood keyed to clan, deepened by drinking resonant blood rather than studied from books - powers as heritage, not curriculum.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.life_force_casting: Nearly every vampiric power costs a Rouse Check - roll one die, and on a 5 or less Hunger rises - so the engine of all magic is literally the caster's own blood.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.ritual_casting: Blood Sorcery Rituals are lengthy ceremonies requiring ingredients, time, and rolls beyond in-the-moment powers, letting Tremere and other sorcerers trade preparation for effects no Discipline click can match.
vector_explanations.meta.safety.lines_and_veils: The corebook formally addresses its mature content with safety guidance - session-zero calibration, lines and veils, and check-in techniques - published as part of playing the game correctly.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.wound_levels: Health boxes take Superficial damage (halved, marked /) or Aggravated (marked X); filling the tracker leaves a vampire Impaired and then in torpor, a short graded ladder rather than a big HP pool.
vector_explanations.social.reputation.faction_standing: Status is a rated Advantage measuring standing within the Camarilla or Anarch Movement, with sect membership shaping access, boons, and the price of breaching the Masquerade.
vector_explanations.narrative.worldbuilding.community_creati: Coterie creation is a structured group exercise: pick a coterie type (Cerberus, Champions, Watchmen), pool dots into shared Domain traits - Chasse, Lien, Portillon - and buy communal coterie Backgrounds.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.standard_array: Attributes use a fixed distribution - one at 4, three at 3, four at 2, one at 1 - assigned where the player chooses, guaranteeing comparable coteries without point math.

title: Chronicles of Darkness
resolution_core: d10 pool (attribute + skill), 8+ = success, 10s reroll (10-again); zero pools roll a single chance die

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.dice_pool_succe: Actions roll attribute plus skill in d10s, counting every 8, 9, or 10 as a success; one success completes most tasks, with the pool size doing the heavy lifting of competence.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.exploding_dice: 10-again is the default: every 10 rerolls for potential extra successes, and equipment or powers can upgrade pools to 9-again, 8-again, or the rote quality's blanket rerolls.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_failure: When modifiers empty a pool you roll a single chance die - success only on a 10, dramatic failure on a 1 - and players may voluntarily downgrade any failure to a dramatic failure to bank a Beat.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.degrees_of_success: Five or more successes on one roll is an exceptional success, which by rule grants a beneficial Condition such as Inspired or Steadfast on top of achieving the goal.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.willpower_spend: Spending Willpower adds three dice to an action (or +2 to a resistance trait), the system's universal clutch resource for moments that matter.
vector_explanations.mental.composure.willpower_recovery: Willpower refreshes through rest and through roleplaying anchors - fulfilling your Virtue restores the whole pool once per chapter, indulging your Vice returns a point per scene.
vector_explanations.character.identity.values_drives: Every character records Aspirations - short- and long-term intentions like 'protect my sister from the truth' - and fulfilling one is a codified Beat trigger steering play toward personal stakes.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.story_xp_triggers: Advancement runs on Beats earned from fiction: resolving Conditions, dramatic failures, fulfilled Aspirations, and session ends, with five Beats converting to one Experience.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Experiences are spent at fixed costs to raise attributes, skills, and Merits between chapters, a slow purchase economy layered under the Beat engine.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Merits - Resources, Striking Looks, Trained Observer, fighting styles - are dot-rated perks bought with Experiences, and the Sanctity of Merits rule refunds dots when the story strips them away.
vector_explanations.mental.composure.emotional_states: Conditions such as Shaken, Spooked, Guilty, and Swooning are defined states with explicit effects and a printed resolution trigger that pays a Beat when the character confronts or succumbs to them.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: Combat applies Tilts - Arm Wrack, Blinded, Knocked Down, Insensate - a standardized list of battlefield states with precise rules for infliction and recovery, kept separate from story Conditions.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.breaking_point: Witnessing or committing the unbearable forces a breaking point roll (Resolve + Composure with situational modifiers); failure inflicts Conditions like Guilty, Shaken, or Broken and can erode Integrity itself.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.corruption_track: Integrity runs 1-10 and falls through failed breaking points as the supernatural grinds a person down, with lower Integrity making every future breaking point roll harder.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.amnesia_fugues: Persistent Conditions like Amnesia and Fugue blank memory or hand the Storyteller control during blackouts, mechanizing lost time as a lasting affliction rather than a scene effect.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: Nine attributes in three categories are bought by prioritizing 5/4/3 dots across Mental, Physical, and Social columns, the classic Storyteller allocation.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_ranks: Skills are rated 0-5 dots across the same three categories with an 11/7/4 priority spread, added to attributes to form every dice pool.
vector_explanations.character.skills.specialty_trees: Skill Specialties - Persuasion (Fast Talk), Firearms (Shotguns) - add a die whenever the narrower niche applies, layering expertise beneath broad skills.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_defaults: Rolling a skill at 0 dots is allowed at a penalty - minus three dice for Mental skills, minus one for Physical and Social - so ignorance is graded, not prohibitive.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is 1d10 plus Dexterity plus Composure rolled at the start of combat, quickness and cool jointly setting the order.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.wound_levels: Health is a short track marked with bashing, lethal, and aggravated damage in escalating symbols, with worse damage overwriting lesser and the track's end meaning collapse or death.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.death_spiral: The final three Health boxes impose -1, -2, and -3 wound penalties to all actions, so the badly hurt slide mechanically toward their end - grim realism by design.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.extended_tests: Extended actions accumulate successes across rolls toward a target, with the number of allowed rolls capped by attribute plus skill and each failure tempting a Condition to finish faster.
vector_explanations.social.structure.social_combat_tracks: Social maneuvering opens Doors: a resistance track set by the target's traits, lowered roll by roll at a pace set by your impression level, until the target's Doors are open and the ask succeeds.

title: Unknown Armies
resolution_core: d100 roll-under skill; obsession and passions allow flip-flopping the dice

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.roll_under_perc: Everything resolves on d100 against percentile skills sitting under four stats; roll at or under to succeed, in a stripped-down occult-underground take on percentile play.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.opposed_rolls: Contested actions have both sides roll under their skills with the higher successful roll prevailing, so being good matters most when someone equally desperate is rolling back.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_success: An 01 is a critical success and 00 a horrid fumble, with matched doubles carrying special weight - the game reads the dice themselves for texture beyond pass-fail.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.multi_axis_madness_meters: The signature Madness Meters track five parallel gauges - Violence, the Unnatural, Helplessness, Isolation, and Self - each with its own Hardened and Failed notches, so trauma is typed, not generic.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.hardened_desensitization: Passing a stress check adds a Hardened notch that inures you to that category forever after; max out a meter's Hardened track and you are calm, functional, and no longer entirely a person.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.fear_tests: Shocks are ranked 1-10 per meter; when a shock's rank exceeds your Hardened notches you make a stress check, and failure adds a Failed notch while forcing panic - fight, flight, or freeze.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.disorders_treatment: Failed notches can be slowly worked off through therapy and stress-free downtime, but a meter that accumulates five Failed notches saddles the character with a lasting mental illness tied to that trauma type.
vector_explanations.character.progression.obsession: Every character names an obsession and one obsession skill, which may flip-flop its rolls (swap tens and ones digits) - identity, drive, and mechanical edge fused into one choice.
vector_explanations.mental.composure.passion_inspiration_ris: Rage, Fear, and Noble passions are written stimuli; when a scene engages one, you may flip-flop a relevant roll once per session per passion, letting emotion produce inspired performance.
vector_explanations.character.identity.values_drives: Character definition is interrogative: before numbers matter you must state what obsesses you and what enrages, terrifies, and ennobles you, and those written answers are what the mechanics hook into.
vector_explanations.character.skills.freeform_skills: Skills are player-authored and named ('Drive Like a Maniac 40%', 'Notice Bullshit 30%'), rated in percentiles under their parent stats and interpreted by the table.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: A fixed pool of points is split among Body, Speed, Mind, and Soul, which serve directly as roll-under targets and as the ceilings for the skills written beneath them.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.belief_paradigms: Postmodern magick runs on paradigm: each adept school (Dipsomancy, Plutomancy, Pornomancy) is built on a central paradox and its own rules for what reality will permit its believers.
vector_explanations.magic.risk.taboo_restrictions: Every school carries a taboo - a dipsomancer who sobers up, a plutomancer who spends his hoard - and violating it immediately destroys all stored charges, binding power to compulsive behavior.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.mana_points: Adepts bank minor, significant, and major charges generated by school-specific obsessive acts (getting drunk, hoarding cash) and spend them on spells with listed charge costs - a fuel economy of compulsion.
vector_explanations.character.identity.splat_membership: Occult identity is a template choice: adepts belong to a school of magick while avatars channel archetypes of the Invisible Clergy (the Pilgrim, the Warrior), each with unique powers, taboos, and channels.
vector_explanations.combat.melee.unarmed: Hand-to-hand attacks deal damage equal to the sum of the roll's digits (a successful 47 deals 11), giving brawls their own arithmetic distinct from weapons and making fights ugly but survivable.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Wound points equal the Body stat and damage subtracts directly; firearms deal the whole successful roll as damage, which is precisely why the book keeps warning you not to bring guns into anything.

title: Night's Black Agents
resolution_core: GUMSHOE: investigative clues are automatic; general tests roll 1d6 + pool spends vs Difficulty (usually 4)

vector_explanations.simulation.investigation.gumshoe_auto_cl: Any agent with the right investigative ability (Tradecraft, Vampirology, Forgery) automatically gets every core clue; pool spends buy extra intel, favors, and dramatic advantages on top.
vector_explanations.character.progression.gumshoe_points: Agents are built from build points and spend earned points between operations to deepen investigative and general pools - burned-spy competence measured in refreshable reservoirs.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.static_dc: General tests roll 1d6 plus points spent from the relevant pool against Difficulty 4 by default, so the drama is in how much of a finite pool you commit before the die falls.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.no_attributes: True to GUMSHOE, there are no attributes: an agent is a set of rated investigative and general abilities, tuned here for Jason Bourne competence levels.
vector_explanations.politics.intrigue.conspiramid: The GM builds the vampire conspiracy as the Conspyramid - six levels of nodes from street-level assets up to the masterminds - and play is literally the agents climbing and burning it node by node.
vector_explanations.simulation.faction.adversaries: The vampires themselves are GM-constructed from modular power and bane menus (supernatural, damned, alien, or mutant builds), and the Vampyramid flowchart scripts how the conspiracy escalates its counterattacks.
vector_explanations.stealth.pursuit.chase_rules: Thriller chase rules run pursuit as opposed contests with a Lead that raises and drops, covering foot, car, and boat pursuits with maneuvers, hazards, and dramatic reversals until escape or capture.
vector_explanations.combat.melee.scuffling: Hand-to-hand runs on the Scuffling pool spent to boost d6 attack rolls, and Thriller Combat options let high-rated fighters unlock extra attacks, disarms, and martial-arts cherries.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.called_shots: Called shots are codified with difficulty bumps for targeting heads, hands, or the stake through the heart - essential in a game where anatomy matters to killing the undead.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Health is a pool: below 0 the agent is hurt and penalized, at -6 seriously wounded and rolling for consciousness, at -12 dead - the GUMSHOE damage ladder tuned for gunfights.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.stability_points: Stability is a spendable, testable pool depleted by violence and the supernatural; hitting bottom risks lasting psychological damage, and agents can burn it deliberately to fuel desperate efforts.
vector_explanations.character.identity.bonds_relationships: Each agent names Sources of Stability - a Symbol, a Solace, and a Safety - written people and anchors that restore Stability and that the vampires are explicitly encouraged to target.
vector_explanations.social.relationships.loyalty_scores: Trust is rated between agents and is spendable currency - stake Trust for mechanical benefit, or in Mirror-mode games betray it for a payoff - quantifying whether the team survives contact with the conspiracy.
vector_explanations.social.influence.cover_identities: Cover is a pool of maintained false identities; each legend spent grants access and protection until it is burned, whereupon the conspiracy starts pulling that thread.
vector_explanations.narrative.authority.fact_declaration: The Network pool lets a player declare a contact into existence anywhere on Earth - spend points and the old Moscow fixer you never mentioned is suddenly canon, rated by the points spent.
vector_explanations.downtime.structure.reducing_heat: Heat measures how much attention mayhem has drawn from police and the conspiracy; it climbs with public violence and is reduced by lying low, burning covers, and leaving the city.
vector_explanations.narrative.tone.tone_dials: Four explicit campaign modes - Burn, Dust, Mirror, and Stakes - toggle rules modules for psychological cost, gritty realism, paranoia about loyalty, and moral horror before play begins.
vector_explanations.equipment.economy_of_gear.quick_equipmen: The Preparedness ability tests whether the agent packed exactly the right gear all along, retroactively declaring equipment mid-mission in place of shopping-list logistics.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.group_checks: Piggybacking lets the best operative roll a single test for group infiltration while every rider spends a point from the same ability, formalizing the team moving as one shadow.
vector_explanations.luck.mitigation.guaranteed_minimum_resul: Each agent's MOS (Military Occupational Specialty) designates one ability in which they may simply declare success once per session - a floor of pure professional reliability.
